stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
In this image of a typical FMA why is "VOR/LOC” in a smaller font of a different colour?

options.a: To help them distinguish between engaged modes and armed modes. MCP SPD HDG and ALT are engaged, and VOR/LOC is armed.
options.b: Because all modes are in a small font when first selected. After 5 seconds the font changes and the caption replaces the last mode in use in this example HDG.
options.c: Because modes selected by buttons on the MCP are always in a large green font Managed modes selected via the FMC are in a small white font until the programmed parameter is achieved, when they change to a large white font.
options.d: Because modes based on ground radio navigation facilities are in a different font size to remind the pilot that they need to be tuned before use.
